data.description:
## Why
Sharper detection content in criteria that already exist -- pure prompt work, findings get sharper, generic passes untouched.

## What
- E6: verify-command defect taxonomy (nine ways a present command lies) + corrected-command calibration anchors (word-boundary/quoted-key grep; jq -e cardinality; assert on structured artifact not prose; prerequisite ordering; etc.).
- T3 (G-8, T3-LOCAL): per-command --help empiricism, endpoint granularity, environment-precondition probes; four-class evidence classification (Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted) recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] + sidecar. ZERO Pass-2 change (generic likelihood grader reads richer evidence); spike stays in Pass-4 moves.
- G3 (G-11): three-part coverage standard (same observable outcome / scope matching-or-exceeding / measurable in same terms; "when in doubt, partial") + name bypass-annotation / scope-narrowing / partial-without-remainder. (Scope-gap already covered by G3.coverage_map.)
- T5c/T10 (FP-3a): ambiguity-not-absence access framing (undeclared posture is the finding; drops the FP where the plan deliberately chose+stated public/permissive).
- T11: assert post-rollback SCHEMA STATE (not just rollback exit 0); ambiguous destructiveness -> literal DATA LOSS RISK note.
- E2: coach "state the default in the plan" for defaultable gaps.
- G4: consumer-enumeration precursor (enumerate consumers first, then check the existing consumer-impact mode -- recall->worklist).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each criterion rubric updated as above; CI prompt/routing drift-parity stays green
- [ ] T3 four-class scale confined to T3 Pass-1 + sidecar; Pass 2/4 unchanged
- [ ] REJECTED here: T9 RETIRE-row rule (nit)
data.title: WS5: Rubric enrichments in existing criteria (prompt-only)
